zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes de Stafford於1114年出生在英國維爾爾時，她的父親Nicholas de Stafford40歲，母親Maud Moolte37歲。她於1149年與Robert de Stourton結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1150年之後在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes de Stafford于1114年出生在英国維爾爾时，她的父亲Nicholas de Stafford40岁，母亲Maud Moolte37岁。她于1149年与Robert de Stourton结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1150年之后在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Stafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Stafford (Staffordshire), but occasionally from other places with similar names, such as Stafford House in Ifield (Sussex), possibly East and West Stowford in East Down (Devon), and three minor places in Devon called Stafford, in Dolton, Broadhembury, and Colyton parishes. The places are all named with Old English ford ‘ford’ as the final element, but have different initial elements. The Staffordshire placename has Old English stæth ‘river bank, shore’; Stafford in Colyton (Devon) has Old English stān ‘stone, rock’; the Sussex placename has Old English stēor ‘steer, bullock’; East and West Stowford (Devon) have Old English stæf ‘staff, stave, rod’; Stafford in Dolton and Stafford in Broadhembury (Devon) may have Old English stæth, stān, or stæf.

variants.name: Stocker
meanings: German and Swiss German (also Stöcker): topographic name for someone living by a tree stump (see Stock 3) or an occupational name for a tree cutter, from Middle High German stocken ‘to clear land’.
German and Swiss German (also Stöcker); Dutch: occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Dutch stocker ‘jailer’.

English: occupational name from Middle English stokker ‘one who sells stockfish’ (fish dried in the air without salt). This was the usual source of the name in medieval London, where a bylaw of 1419 stated that no stokker should board a ship to buy fish (presumably in order to forestall the market).

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes de Stafford was born in 1114, in Wiltshire, England, her father, Nicholas de Stafford, was 40 and her mother, Maud Moolte, was 37. She married Robert de Stourton in 1149. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died after 1150, in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes de Stafford est née en 1114, à Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Nicholas de Stafford, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Maud Moolte, avait 37 ans. Elle a épousé Robert de Stourton en 1149. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 1150, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes de Stafford 1114 in Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Nicholas de Stafford 40 und ihre Mutter Maud Moolte 37. Sie hat 1149 Robert de Stourton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 1150 in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Stafford è nata il nel 1114 nel Wiltshire, England, sua padre, Nicholas de Stafford, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Maud Moolte, aveva 37 anni. Lei sposato Robert de Stourton nel 1149. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1150, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes de Staffordが1114年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアで生まれた時，父Nicholas de Staffordは40歳，母Maud Moolteは37歳であった。1149年にRobert de Stourtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1150年以後に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes de Stafford(이)가 1114년 영국 윌츠히르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas de Stafford(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Moolte(은)는 37세였다. 그녀는 1149년 Robert de Stourton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1150년 이후 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Stafford nasceu em 1114, em Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Nicholas de Stafford, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Maud Moolte, tinha 37 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert de Stourton em 1149. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 1150, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes de Stafford родилась в 1114 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия, её отцу по имени Nicholas de Stafford было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Moolte, 37. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert de Stourton в 1149 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла после 1150 г. в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes de Stafford nació en 1114, en Wiltshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Nicholas de Stafford, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Maud Moolte, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Robert de Stourton en 1149. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió después de 1150, en su pueblo.
